title: Savory Venezuelan Mincemeat Empanadas
description: Buttery and pillowy empanadas filled with mincemeat, perfect for an adventurous meal, served in a terracotta dish
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent.
Stir in the mincemeat and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Brush the edges of each dough circle with the brine.
Place a tablespoon of the beef and mincemeat mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the egg wash and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place the empanadas in a terracotta dish and brush with melted butter.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
